channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated from multiple sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Launches Major Missile and Drone Attack on Multiple Ukrainian Cities, Injuring 14
item.description: Russia struck multiple Ukrainian cities overnight with missiles and drones, injuring at least 14 people. The large-scale attack continues the pattern of systematic strikes on Ukrainian civilian infrastructure.

item.title: 🇺🇳 NATO Official: Trump Cannot Expel Members Over Iran War Stance
item.description: A NATO official confirmed that the US cannot unilaterally expel alliance members for refusing to back the US-Israeli war against Iran. The statement comes amid Pentagon discussions about punishing non-cooperative NATO allies.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland's Tusk Questions US Loyalty to NATO Alliance
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk has publicly questioned whether the United States remains committed to NATO, reflecting growing European anxiety about US reliability as a security partner.

item.title: ✈️ Ukraine's An-28 Turboprop Now Launches Interceptor Drones to Hunt Russian UAVs
item.description: Ukraine has upgraded its An-28 Cash counter-drone platform to launch interceptor drones alongside its Minigun armament, marking a significant evolution in Ukrainian air defense capabilities.

item.title: 🇮🇹 Italy Scraps Billion Defense Deficit Spending Amid Economic Concerns
item.description: Italy has abandoned plans for 14 billion euros in deficit spending on defense, citing competing priorities including energy costs. The decision raises questions about European defense spending commitments.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳🇮🇷 US Sanctions Major Chinese Refinery and 40 Shippers Over Iranian Oil
item.description: The Trump administration imposed sanctions on a major China-based oil refinery (Hengli Petrochemical) and roughly 40 shipping companies involved in transporting Iranian oil. The move implements secondary sanctions threats and comes weeks before Trump meets Xi Jinping in China.

item.title: 🇨🇴🇻🇪 Colombia's Petro Becomes First Leader to Visit Venezuela Post-Maduro Abduction
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro met interim Venezuelan President Delcy Rodriguez in Caracas, becoming the first foreign leader to visit since the US military abducted Nicolas Maduro in January. Border security and US-Colombia tensions dominated discussions.

item.title: 🇽🇰🇷🇸 Kosovo Court Jails Three Serbs for Life Over 2023 Banjska Attack
item.description: A court in Pristina sentenced two ethnic Serbs to life in prison and a third to 30 years on terrorism charges for the 2023 armed attack in Banjska that killed one police officer and three gunmen. The attack was the worst since Kosovo declared independence in 2008.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱🇺🇳 UN Warns Israeli Attacks on Lebanon May Violate International Law
item.description: A UN human rights report found that Israeli strikes on residential buildings in Lebanon and Hezbollah rocket fire into Israel may constitute serious violations of international humanitarian law. Over 2,400 people have been killed in Lebanon since the escalation began.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Admin Moves Again to End Legal Status of CBP One Migrants
item.description: The Trump administration announced plans to again terminate the temporary legal status of hundreds of thousands who entered the US via the CBP One app, despite a previous court ruling blocking the move. About 900,000 people were granted humanitarian parole under the Biden-era program.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu treated for prostate cancer, Israeli PM reveals
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu announced he was successfully treated for prostate cancer, revealing a previously undisclosed diagnosis. The news comes amid ongoing tensions with Iran and domestic political challenges.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Justice Department drops criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
item.description: The Justice Department has dropped its criminal investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over renovation costs. The move likely clears the way for Kevin Warsh to potentially succeed Powell.

item.title: 🇸🇾 Suspected leader of notorious Tadamon massacre arrested in Syria
item.description: Amjad Youssef, wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 during the Syrian civil war, has been arrested. The Tadamon massacre was one of the conflict most notorious atrocities.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU leaders activate mutual defence pact amid Trump NATO threats
item.description: European leaders are looking to a little-known mutual assistance clause in EU treaties as Donald Trump threatens NATO allies. The move reflects growing concerns about US commitment to European security.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian soldiers emaciated from lack of food and water on frontlines
item.description: Ukrainian troops are suffering from severe malnutrition as supply lines falter. Reports describe soldiers left in critical condition from inadequate food and water supplies at the front.

item.title: 🤖 Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots for frontline logistics
item.description: Ukraine plans to deploy 25,000 unmanned ground vehicles to replace soldiers in dangerous logistics missions. Ground robots ran over 9,000 frontline missions in March alone, triple November count.

item.title: 🚢 Three US carriers operate in Middle East for first time since 2003
item.description: Three US aircraft carriers are now operating in Central Command area of responsibility simultaneously, reflecting the massive naval buildup amid the Iran conflict.

item.title: ☢️ G7 expresses concern over Russia and China nuclear build-up
item.description: G7 nations voice alarm at expanding nuclear arsenals of Russia and China ahead of month-long NPT review conference. China is rapidly expanding its warhead stockpile while Russia modernizes delivery systems.

item.title: 🇸🇪 NATO eyes Swedish-Canadian jet for AWACS role, shifting from Boeing
item.description: NATO is considering Saab GlobalEye aircraft for airborne early warning missions, moving away from Boeing platforms. Political momentum has been building for the Swedish-Canadian solution.

item.title: ⚡ US military pursues fleet of missile-killing laser drones
item.description: The US military is reviving directed energy weapons programs for airborne platforms to counter missile threats. The initiative aims to protect American airspace from evolving aerial threats.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK affirms Falklands sovereignty after reported US review
item.description: Downing Street says Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK after an internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a change in position in retaliation for Britain not joining the Iran war.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU opens anti-fraud investigation into UK ambassador Mandelson
item.description: The European Anti-Fraud Office confirms it has sufficient information to launch an investigation into Peter Mandelson, the UK ambassador to the US, though it did not specify if fraud is involved.

item.title: 💰 P&G warns of $1 billion profit hit from Iran war oil prices
item.description: Procter & Gamble warns higher oil prices driven by the Iran conflict will cost the company approximately $1 billion in reduced profit for fiscal 2027.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany halves 2026 growth forecast as Iran war raises inflation
item.description: The German government has cut its 2026 growth forecast in half and raised inflation expectations, citing economic disruption from the Iran conflict and elevated energy costs.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK warns of rising state-backed cyberattacks as AI accelerates threats
item.description: Britain must prepare for increased state-sponsored cyberattacks, with security officials warning that AI is speeding up the evolution of cyber threats.
